steps.title: Substitute the expression for t
steps.content:
Start with $p^{\frac{5}{4}} = t^{\frac{7}{4}}$ and the relation $t = p^{2n-1}$. Substitute the expression for $t$:
$$p^{\frac{5}{4}} = (p^{2n-1})^{\frac{7}{4}}$$

steps.title: Apply power rule and equate exponents
steps.content:
Simplify the right side using the power rule $(a^m)^k = a^{mk}$:
$$p^{\frac{5}{4}} = p^{\frac{7(2n-1)}{4}}$$

Since the bases are equal and $p > 1$, the exponents must be equal:
$$\frac{5}{4} = \frac{7(2n-1)}{4}$$

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by 4:
$$5 = 7(2n-1)$$

Distribute:
$$5 = 14n - 7$$

Add 7 to both sides:
$$12 = 14n$$

Divide by 14:
$$n = \frac{12}{14} = \frac{6}{7}$$
